rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH THEW ET AL VS. PAUL TUEN WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2019 line 8. Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction GRANTED as follows: Defendant is enjoined from illegally severing or interfering with Plaintiff's alleged housing services: storage space and parking. For the duration of the injunction Plaintiff shall not be requested to decrease the storage space (parties to present to the Court at the hearing the dimensions of the space historically used for storage within the garage) or to refrain from parking in the driveway or garage, one of these spaces shall be at all times exclusively provided to Plaintiff and Plaintiff shall not be required to make herself available to move the car for someone else to be able to park and her car shall not be blocked by any other cars. Defendant is not precluded from asserting his legal claims concerning the disputed parking and storage area. Defendant may bring a motion to lift the injunction if and when Defendant obtains information concerning the possibility of using the garage for both (1) storage; and (2) side -by-side parking of two cars; or (1) storage and (2) parking one car in the garage and Plaintiff's car in the driveway (historical parking space). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH M KLINE VS. JOHN FOECKE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANT JOHN FOECKE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege: (1) that Plaintiff resides in the same rental unit with her tenant; and (2) that the grounds stated in the notice (37.9(b)) is the dominant motive for recovering possession. "In any action to recover possession of the rental unit under Section 37.9, the landlord must plead and prove that at least one of the grounds enumerated in Section 37.9(a) or (b) and also stated in the notice to vacate is the dominant motive for recovering possession." = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY DER VS. NG PEI SZE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY/RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2019 line 7. PLAINTIFF HENRY DER's MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY/RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Request for production within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $960 to be paid no later than April 2, 2019. = (501/REQ)
